title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s across multiple team members definitely points to something systemic rather than a permissions issue on your end.

To start narrowing this down: do you know if any deployments, infrastructure changes, or configuration updates were made to TaskFlow around the time this started (~2 days ago)? For example, updates to the auth service, reverse proxy/load balancer config, or session management? If you're not sure, could you check with whoever manages deploys and let me know what went out in that window?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm not sure about the exact details, but I do know there was some kind of update a couple days ago — I think they added a new role or something? I was actually just assigned the 'analyst' role around that time. I don't manage the deployments myself so I can't tell you exactly what changed on the infrastructure side. You'd have to check with the ops team for specifics. I just know that's roughly when the 403s started showing up for me.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: That's really helpful — the timing of the new 'analyst' role lines up perfectly with when the 403s started. Quick follow-up: do you know if the other team members who are also seeing 403s were assigned the 'analyst' role too? And when you do get a successful load vs. a 403, is it the entire dashboard that fails, or are there specific pages or actions (e.g., viewing reports, editing tasks) that seem to trigger the 403 more than others?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Now that you mention it, I think the ones who mentioned having the same problem were also recently given the analyst role. My colleague Sarah who's an editor said she hasn't had any issues, so maybe it is related to that role specifically? As for what fails — when it happens, the whole page just comes back as 403. It's not like some parts load and others don't. It's all or nothing. And honestly it feels kind of random, like maybe 1 out of every 3 times I refresh it fails. I assumed it was some kind of network hiccup but maybe not.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks, that's a strong signal — the 'analyst' role correlation is almost certainly the cause. The piece I'm trying to figure out now is *why it's intermittent* rather than failing every time. A simple missing permission would block you 100% of the time, not ~1 in 3.

Could you try something for me? Next time you hit a 403, **refresh the page 5-6 times in quick succession** and note roughly how many succeed vs. fail. I'm trying to determine if the pattern is truly random per-request (which would point to a load balancer or caching issue) or if there's a time-based pattern.

Also: when you *do* get a 403, does logging out and back in fix it temporarily, or is the very next request after re-login also a coin flip?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
I just tried that actually — refreshed a bunch of times in a row. Out of 6 refreshes, 2 of them gave me 403s. So yeah it does seem pretty consistent, like roughly every third try fails. It doesn't seem time-based, more like each request is a coin flip.

As for logging out and back in — I've tried that and it doesn't really help. I log back in and it's the same thing, sometimes it works and sometimes it doesn't. The next request after logging in is just as likely to be a 403 as any other time.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden for users with 'analyst' role — likely inconsistent role config across load-balanced instances","problem":"Users assigned the newly introduced 'analyst' role receive 403 Forbidden errors on approximately 1 out of every 3 requests to the dashboard. The entire page fails (not partial). Users with other roles (e.g., 'editor') are unaffected. The issue began when the 'analyst' role was deployed ~2 days ago. Re-authentication does not resolve it.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The new 'analyst' role permissions were not consistently applied across all backend/application server instances behind the load balancer. One (or more) instances is missing the role definition or its associated permission grants. Requests routed to the misconfigured instance(s) return 403; requests to correctly configured instances succeed. The ~33% failure rate suggests 1 of 3 instances is affected.","reproduction_steps":["Log in as a user with the 'analyst' role","Navigate to the TaskFlow dashboard","Refresh the page 6+ times in quick succession","Observe that approximately 1 in 3 requests returns a 403 Forbidden error","Repeat with a user who has the 'editor' role — no 403s should occur"],"environment":"Production TaskFlow deployment, load-balanced across multiple backend instances. New 'analyst' role was deployed approximately 2 days ago. Exact infrastructure details (number of instances, load balancer type) need to be confirmed with the ops team.","severity":"high","impact":"All users assigned the 'analyst' role are intermittently locked out of the dashboard (~33% of requests fail). Multiple team members are affected. No workaround exists — logging out and back in does not help.","recommended_fix":"1. Check all backend/application server instances for consistent 'analyst' role configuration — compare role definitions, permission grants, and any related RBAC/authorization config across instances. 2. Identify which instance(s) are missing or have stale config (likely a missed deployment, failed config propagation, or cache inconsistency). 3. Redeploy or sync the role configuration to all instances. 4. Verify fix by confirming the 'analyst' role exists and has dashboard access permissions on every instance. 5. Review deployment process to ensure role/permission changes are applied atomically across all instances.","proposed_test_case":"As a user with the 'analyst' role, make 20 sequential requests to the dashboard endpoint and assert all return 200. Run this against each backend instance individually (bypassing the load balancer) to confirm per-instance correctness.","information_gaps":["Exact number of backend instances and load balancer configuration (ops team has this)","Whether the role was deployed via a migration, config file, or admin UI — and whether the deployment tool reported success on all instances","Exact server-side error logs corresponding to the 403 responses (would confirm whether it's a missing role, missing permission grant, or authorization cache issue)"]}
